Subject: Short window patch required for scheduler jitter (customer-facing note drafted)

Team —

We found a narrow but reproducible regression in the...
text:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-11-14T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Short window patch required for scheduler jitter (customer-facing note drafted)

Team —

We found a narrow but reproducible regression in the request scheduler that intermittently increases tail latency when clients send many concurrent short streaming requests (multi-token streaming bursts). Root cause: race in the slot reclamation path under high concurrent interleaving. Impact is limited but visible on a subset of customers (notably Acme Retail) as 200–400ms spikes on tail p50->p99 for short requests.

Planned immediate mitigation: a lightweight hotfix to force a deterministic slot release path and tighten the reclamation lock window. This is a short, in-process patch (no model reload) that we can deploy clus...

title: NOVAtech: sustained inference slowdowns after canary - timeline & ask
preview:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Alex —

We started seeing a significant degradation in inference performance across several endpoints shortly after your te...
text:
From: Sanjay Rao <sanjay@novatech.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 9 Nov 2028 15:12:00 -0800
Subject: NOVA - sudden increase in latency and errors since last canary

Alex —

We started seeing a significant degradation in inference performance across several endpoints shortly after your team flipped the canary yesterday afternoon. Our P95 jumped from ~110ms to ~480ms and P99s are consistently 1.4–1.9s. Error rates climbed from 0.2% to ~3% during heavy tails. This is impacting synchronous search flows; downstream timeouts have triggered customer complaints.

We rolled back our own client-side retries but the issue tracks to your hosted endpoints (region us-west2). The spike aligns with the canary timestamp: Nov 9 14:40 PST. Attached are a small sample of traces and our aggregated latency metrics.

We need an immediate mitigation plan and timeline for a rollba...

Impact is visible as increased error rates and timeouts for requests that traverse those peers — primarily metadata and webhook egress used by some customers.

What we know so far:
- Start time: ~2028-07-21T08:40:00Z.
- Affected regions: us-east (primary) and eu-west (secondary).

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.ai>

Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 07 Jun 2027 14:05:00 -0700
Subject: RE: Discovery — embeddings + rerank + streaming funcalls (post-call recap)

Alex — thanks again for taking the call today. Quick recap while it's fresh and a proposed set of decisions so we can lock an implementation path. TL;DR: you want a deterministic rerank/embedding pipeline for patient record retrieval, predictable latency for 95th pct, and a lightweight function-calling surface for structured output to downstream EHR processors.

Key pain points you mentioned:
- Search recall variability across long clinical notes (embeddings drift across updates).
- Token-level cost spikes during peak triage windows (evening PST).
- Need for a single, auditable structured output schema for downstream ingestion into your CDW.
